cards.title: Proposal Form & FPR
cards.content:
ΓÇó Proposal Form: The primary document containing all material facts. The basic foundation of the contract.
ΓÇó First Premium Receipt (FPR): Serves as evidence that the policy contract has commenced and risk is covered.

cards.title: KYC & AML
cards.content:
ΓÇó KYC: Know Your Customer (Identity & Address proof) is mandatory to prevent frauds.
ΓÇó AML: Anti-Money Laundering guidelines prevent illegal funds from entering the financial system.

items.text: Standard (Passport, School Cert) vs Non-Standard (Horoscope, Gram Panchayat cert).
Medical Examiner's Report: Required if the sum assured is high or age is advanced.
e-Insurance Account (e-IA): Used to hold policies electronically through an Insurance Repository.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: If a non-standard age proof is submitted, the insurer usually charges an extra premium (age proof loading).
Agent's Confidential Report: The agent must submit a moral hazard report about the prospect's lifestyle and income.
Renewal Premium Receipt (RPR): Proof that the policy is active and subsequent premiums are paid.
e-Insurance eliminates the risk of losing physical policy documents.
PAN card is mandatory for cash premiums exceeding Γé╣50,000.
